summary: Justin Brennan teaches a five-step apartment buying framework focusing on deal-sourcing and underwriting. He emphasizes that success in multifamily investing requires finding good deals first (through broker relationships and on-to-off-market strategies), analyzing them against specific return thresholds (14-20% IRR, 6-10% cash-on-cash, 1.8x equity multiple), and structuring deals with proper general partner and limited partner arrangements. He provides a detailed case study showing how a $188,000 investment in a 31-unit deal generated nearly $600,000 in returns across multiple fee sources.
takeaways: Target 10-30 unit deals for your first multifamily investment, not 50+ units—this is where 'the numbers make sense' and you can actually execute
Use broker relationships instead of direct-to-seller cold outreach: 'for every phone call you're making a broker and his team are making five'
The deal sequence is 'deal, debt, equity'—find a good deal first, then financing, then raise equity capital; never chase money without a deal
Acquisition fees are typically 1-3% of purchase price; asset management fees are 1-2% of monthly revenue; disposition fees are 1-2% of sale price
On a $1.6M deal with $510K total equity needed, bring in 10% ($50K) out of pocket and raise the remaining 90% ($460K) from investors
Required deal metrics: 14-20% IRR minimum, 6-10%+ cash-on-cash return, 1.8x+ equity multiple—if deals don't meet these thresholds at first glance, 'keep moving'

Get on broker pre-market email lists by building rapport: tour 3 on-market properties with 3 different brokers, then ask 'do you have pre-market deals—we're going to buy here in [market]' to access deals before they're overpriced on websites

skill_candidates.change: Quickly screen deals using three financial metrics (IRR, cash-on-cash, equity multiple) before deep analysis
skill_candidates.evidence: 'the three metrics to the right: you want an IRR of 14 to 20% or more, cash on cash 6 to 10% plus, equity multiple of 1.8x or more...if it doesn't meet those from a high level when you're just moving through deals keep moving right keep moving'

skill_candidates.change: Build broker relationships through sequential property tours and requests for pre-market access rather than cold outreach
skill_candidates.evidence: 'your job is to make a relationship with them to get access to their off-market deals...you go in there and you do that and then now you're starting to build rapport...now you're going to start getting access to their pre-market inventory and that is the stuff that's going to make sense'

skill_candidates.change: Understand NOI (net operating income) as the pre-mortgage profit number that drives deal structuring and investor returns
skill_candidates.evidence: 'you get to that beautiful number that all you've probably heard or maybe not even heard before called noi net operating income and that is the most important number...that's basically your profit before you pay the mortgage on the property'

title: Multifamily Real Estate Investing Tips For Success - Multifamily Property Advice
